# /teams-1on1-notes

Capture 1:1 notes to SharePoint.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

After a 1:1 the notes are half a Teams chat, half memory. This turns them into a consistently structured note, carries forward last session's open items, and files it in the agreed SharePoint spot so a year of 1:1 history is one folder, not forty chat scrolls.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Who the 1:1 was with and the date
- Source material: pasted notes, the Teams meeting chat, or both
- Target location and naming: e.g. SharePoint `…/1on1s/{Name}/{YYYY-MM-DD} - 1on1.docx` - confirm the library and pattern
- Privacy check: is the target library restricted, or team-visible? (feedback notes in a team-visible library is a real incident)
- Standing sections the user wants every time (wins, blockers, growth, actions, parking lot)
- Read-only vs propose changes vs apply after confirmation
- Which required @integration/workspace should be used - see **Connector access**

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Channel-appropriate. Post only after confirmation.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@teams** - Microsoft Teams
  - MCP server key: `microsoft-teams`
  - Example tools: `microsoft-teams__list_channels`, `microsoft-teams__post_message`, `microsoft-teams__list_messages`

- **@sharepoint** - Microsoft SharePoint
  - MCP server key: `microsoft-sharepoint`
  - Example tools: `microsoft-sharepoint__search`, `microsoft-sharepoint__upload_file`, `microsoft-sharepoint__list_sites`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Workflow

1. Gather the raw material - if the source is a Teams meeting chat, pull it via the connector rather than asking the user to paste.
2. Look in the target folder for the previous session's note; extract its open action items and parking-lot topics as carry-forwards.
3. Structure the note: date/attendees header, topics discussed, decisions (quoted, not paraphrased), action items with owner + due date, growth/feedback notes, parking lot for next time, carry-forward status from last session.
4. Mark anything sensitive (performance feedback, comp, personal matters) and confirm the target library's audience is appropriate before filing.
5. Propose the filename per convention and check for a same-date collision.
6. Show the full note for review, then save to SharePoint and return the link.
7. Present a review table of proposed changes before any write/send/create.
8. Apply only after the user says `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3`.

## Output format

**The note itself** - rendered in full for review, in the standing section structure, with carry-forwards marked `↩ from {last date}: {status}`.

**Filing plan** - | Destination path | Filename | Collision? | Library visibility note |

After apply - the SharePoint link, plus next session's seed: open actions and parking lot, ready to paste into the next agenda.

## Do

- Check your AI tool's connector settings first (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings); confirm each required @mention is enabled and authenticated
- Use `search_tool` then `use_tool` with fully-qualified names (`server__tool`)
- Tell the user to attach @teams / @sharepoint in the prompt when the session needs that app
- Carry forward unresolved items from the previous note in the same folder - continuity is the whole point
- Quote decisions and commitments verbatim; paraphrased commitments cause disputes
- Check library visibility before filing anything containing personal feedback
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if critical info is missing

## Don't

- Don't save sensitive feedback to a team-visible library without an explicit go-ahead
- Don't overwrite the previous session's note - every session is a new file
- Don't soften or editorialize what was agreed; record it as said
- Don't invent mail, calendar, or file contents without a successful `use_tool` call
- Don't auto-send, publish, or delete - propose and wait for `apply`
- Don't substitute generic web search when the skill requires a live connector

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@teams, @sharepoint) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)
```

# /teams-channel-digest

Summarize Microsoft Teams channels.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

Back from two days of meetings to 300 unread across six project channels. This reads the channels - including thread replies, where the real decisions hide - and returns: where you were mentioned, what was decided, what's still open, and who owes what. Channels only; private chats are a different surface and stay out unless asked.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Which teams and channels (explicit list, or 'my usual project channels' if established earlier); confirm channels vs chats scope
- Time window (default: since yesterday, or since last digest)
- What to surface: decisions, @mentions of the user, action items, blockers, announcements
- Detail level: headline-only vs per-channel breakdown
- Read-only vs propose changes vs apply after confirmation
- Which required @integration/workspace should be used - see **Connector access**

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Channel-appropriate. Post only after confirmation.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@teams** - Microsoft Teams
  - MCP server key: `microsoft-teams`
  - Example tools: `microsoft-teams__list_channels`, `microsoft-teams__post_message`, `microsoft-teams__list_messages`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Workflow

1. Enumerate the channels in scope and pull root posts **and their thread replies** for the window - a digest built from root posts alone misses most decisions.
2. First pass: find every @mention of the user, what the person needs, and whether someone else already resolved it downthread.
3. Extract per channel: decisions actually made (someone with standing said 'let's do X' and nobody objected ≠ formal, but report it as 'apparent decision'), open questions, action items with owners ('I'll take it' counts), announcements.
4. Collapse the chatter - reactions, +1s, memes, bot noise - into nothing; it doesn't even get a count.
5. Rank channels by signal for this user; channels with nothing notable get one roll-up line.
6. Deep-link every decision, mention, and action item back to its message.
7. This run is read-only: report findings and drafts in chat; do not modify, move, send, or mark anything without a separate confirmed `apply`.

## Output format

**Channel digest - {window}**

**You were mentioned** - first, always: | Channel | By | What they need | Resolved downthread? | Link |

Then per channel (signal-ranked):
**#{channel}** - 2-4 bullet summary
- **Decisions**: each with who + link (marked 'apparent' if informal)
- **Open questions**: unanswered items someone should own
- **Action items**: | Who | What | Due | Link |

**Quiet channels** - one line: "#design, #ops: routine traffic, nothing actionable."​

## Do

- Check your AI tool's connector settings first (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings); confirm each required @mention is enabled and authenticated
- Use `search_tool` then `use_tool` with fully-qualified names (`server__tool`)
- Tell the user to attach @teams in the prompt when the session needs that app
- Read thread replies, not just root posts - decisions live in replies
- Check whether a mention was already handled downthread before flagging it
- Deep-link everything; a digest without links makes the user re-scroll anyway
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if critical info is missing

## Don't

- Don't pull private 1:1/group chats into a channel digest unless explicitly asked
- Don't report a proposal as a decision - distinguish 'X proposed' from 'agreed'
- Don't react, reply, or mark anything read on the user's behalf
- Don't invent mail, calendar, or file contents without a successful `use_tool` call
- Don't auto-send, publish, or delete - propose and wait for `apply`
- Don't substitute generic web search when the skill requires a live connector

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@teams) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)

## Schedule

Recommended cadence: **daily**

Example: `/loop 7d Run /teams-channel-digest`
```

  - MCP server key: `microsoft-teams`
  - Example tools: `microsoft-teams__list_channels`, `microsoft-teams__post_message`, `microsoft-teams__list_messages`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- **Propose before write**: show a review table; wait for user to say `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3`.
- Never delete, send, or publish without explicit confirmation.
- Summarize every action taken at the end of the run so the user can review it.

## Workflow

1. Collect the week's material; when mining channels/mail, prefer shipped artifacts (merged, sent, published) over activity.
2. Fetch last week's status post from the channel for format continuity and to diff against - anything listed 'in progress' last week must resolve to done, still-going (with movement), or honestly stalled.
3. Write outcome-first: 'Shipped X, cutting churn-path clicks from 5→2' beats 'worked on X'. No filler activity.
4. Structure: **TL;DR** (one line) · **Done** · **In progress** (with ETA) · **Blocked** (what + who can unblock + the specific ask) · **Next week**. Cap ~10 lines.
5. Confirm each blocker-mention with the user before including a name.
6. Format for Teams (bold headers, tight bullets, emoji only if channel norm), show the draft, post on apply.
7. Present a review table of proposed changes before any write/send/create.
8. Apply only after the user says `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3`.

## Output format

**Status post draft** - the exact post as a blockquote, Teams-formatted.

**Delta vs last week** - one short table: | Last week said | This week says | - catching anything that silently stalled.

**Blocker confirmations** - list of people who will be publicly named/@mentioned, awaiting the user's yes.

**Posting plan** - channel, timing, mentions. After apply - the post link.

## Do

- Check your AI tool's connector settings first (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings); confirm each required @mention is enabled and authenticated
- Use `search_tool` then `use_tool` with fully-qualified names (`server__tool`)
- Tell the user to attach @teams in the prompt when the session needs that app
- Diff against last week's post so stalled items surface honestly
- Phrase Done items as outcomes with the so-what attached
- Keep the identical section order every week - scanability compounds
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if critical info is missing

## Don't

- Don't pad with meetings-attended filler
- Don't publicly name someone as a blocker without the user's explicit confirm
- Don't carry last week's items forward verbatim without flagging them as stalled
- Don't post before apply
- Don't invent mail, calendar, or file contents without a successful `use_tool` call
- Don't auto-send, publish, or delete - propose and wait for `apply`
- Don't substitute generic web search when the skill requires a live connector

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@teams) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)

## Schedule

Recommended cadence: **weekly**

Example: `/loop 7d Run /teams-post-status`
